narrative: Hormuz geopolitical risks support the dollar as gold finds support above $4,000, while USD/JPY climbs to 162.31 on the back of a +268bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage. The broader market remains steady with gold higher +0.96%, equities +0.00%, DXY +0.20%, and VIX 16.4. USD/JPY options show a strong put skew with 25d RR at -1.67, signaling heavy hedging demand for downside protection despite the spot uptrend. Watch the next RBNZ meeting on July 8 as the next key policy catalyst.
